title: Spectral Estimation (line spectrum / parametric PSD)
sub_domain: Frequency estimation from finite-length time series
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Stoica-Moses 2005 Spectral Analysis of Signals textbook; Kay 1988 Modern Spectral Estimation; Schmidt 1986 MUSIC; Roy-Kailath ESPRIT; Tang-Bhaskar-Shah-Recht 2013 continuous CS for line spectrum.

E.description: A complex-exponential / sinusoidal signal is modeled as sum of K components with unknown frequencies omega_k, amplitudes c_k, and phases phi_k, observed on a finite time window of N samples plus noise; the goal is to estimate (omega_k, c_k, phi_k) via subspace, parametric, or atomic-norm methods.
E.forward_model: y[n] = sum_{k=1}^K c_k * exp(j*omega_k*n + j*phi_k) + w[n],  n = 0,...,N-1

W.regime: Fourier-limited resolution 2pi/N; subspace methods (MUSIC, ESPRIT) achieve super-resolution given SNR; atomic-norm minimization (Tang-Bhaskar-Shah-Recht) guarantees exact recovery when delta_omega > 4pi/N.

C.solver_class: Periodogram/Welch, Yule-Walker AR, MUSIC, ESPRIT, Prony, atomic-norm (AST), sparse BPDN in DFT grid, Nonlinear LS
C.error_bound: CRLB_omega = 6 * sigma^2 / (N^3 * c^2); subspace methods tight at high SNR; periodogram-peak picks resolve to 2pi/N
C.complexity: FFT: O(N*log(N)); MUSIC O(N^3 + N^2*Ngrid); ESPRIT closed-form; atomic SDP O(N^3.5)
